title: X-tra Juicy Ukrainian Duck Braised
description: A hearty, lazy Sunday dish featuring tender duck, braised in a rich, savory sauce with bay leaves and a touch of vinegar. This dish is perfect for a weekend meal with family and friends.
instructions: Season the duck legs with kosher salt and black pepper.
Heat the duck fat or vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the duck legs from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion, carrot, and celery to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the bay leaves and cook for 1 minute.
Add the apple cider vinegar and stir to deglaze the pot.
Add the flour and stir to combine.
Add the white wine and chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Return the duck legs to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the duck for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Skim off any excess fat from the surface of the sauce.
Serve the duck legs with the braising sauce spooned over the top.
Garnish with fresh parsley or thyme, if desired.
